title: Mountain-Rescue Relay Gantry: Minimum-Mass Truss Strut Sizing
strategies: uniform sizing: one cross-section for every strut, just large enough to pass the stress and tip-sway limits (baseline)
one-shot per-bar stress sizing from a single reference FEM solve, then uniform stiffening for the displacement limit
iterative fully-stressed design: re-solve and resize each strut to its redistributed axial force, adding minimal uniform stiffness
sensitivity-based sizing that adds stiffening material where it cuts tip sway per kilogram most
